title: elymus heterophyllus
intro: Elymus heterophyllus, also known as wildrye or blue wildrye, is a perennial grass native to North America. The plant has been used for centuries in traditional medicine, valued for its medicinal properties which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, making it effective in treating wounds, burns, and skin conditions. The health benefits of Elymus heterophyllus include its ability to reduce fever, alleviate respiratory issues, and support the immune system. Herbal preparations of Elymus heterophyllus include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to treat a variety of ailments, and it's also used in poultices for topical applications.
